page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, the system provides 72 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, backed by 36 MB of L3 cache and hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. The hardware configuration is equipped with 576.0 GB of system memory, delivering a balanced 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For high-capacity local storage requirements, the instance includes 45,000 GB of NVMe SSD storage, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 112 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate exceptional performance in memory-bound and storage-intensive operations. The instance achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations on large uncached blocks, as well as in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, which exceed 148 million operations per second. Multi-core processing capabilities are also highly competitive, with top-tier rankings in Geekbench multi-core scores, software compilation using Clang, and multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression. While LLM prompt processing and text generation for medium and large models rank as strong, text generation for very small models represents a performance tradeoff, falling into the lower tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i8ge.18xlarge offers high resource density by combining massive local NVMe storage capacity with energy-efficient ARM architecture. This design reduces the overhead typically associated with external block storage attachments for large-scale data sets. The server is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis caching layers, static web serving, software compilation pipelines, and memory-intensive data processing workloads that benefit from localized high-speed storage.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by a 72-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 576.0 GB of memory, 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 112 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and database transactions. Multi-core benchmarks show strong capabilities in software compilation and multi-threaded decompression, though small-model LLM text generation shows a relative performance tradeoff. The combination of high-capacity local storage and energy-efficient ARM cores provides high resource density for data-intensive applications. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput caching, and memory-intensive data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 72 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 576 GB RAM, 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 72 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 576 GB RAM, and 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density ARM-based storage instance delivering massive local NVMe capacity and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 72-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor
Equipped with 45,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Includes 576.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
Delivers high-throughput performance for Redis and database operations
Features a 112 Gbps baseline network interface on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and data processing
